description:
You are a platform engineer who has just inherited a CI/CD-worker VM that runs scheduled
    pipeline jobs.  
    There is already a directory called 
    /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/raw/   that contains a **static** file named
    snapshot.txt with two previously collected system snapshots.

    Your goal is to generate a deterministic, human-readable
    diagnostic report that a downstream Jenkins step will later archive.
    The downstream step is very strict about the exact file paths,
    the separators that are used, and the numerical precision that
    appears in the report, so please follow the specification below
    exactly.

    1. Create the directory /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/        (it already exists, but create it if necessary).
    2. Read the file /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/raw/snapshot.txt.
       The file is organised as **two blocks**.  
       • Each non-empty line is a KEY=VALUE pair.  
       • Blocks are separated by a single empty line.  
       Keys never repeat inside the same block.  
       Snapshot #1 is always the older one, Snapshot #2 the newer one.
    3. From the two blocks calculate the following deterministic metrics:
       • AVG_CPU_USER           – the arithmetic mean of CPU_USER from both blocks,
                                 rounded to *exactly* two decimals.  
       • PEAK_DISK_USAGE        – the highest value among
                                 DISK_ROOT_USED_PCT, DISK_VAR_USED_PCT and
                                 DISK_HOME_USED_PCT in **either** snapshot,
                                 reported as an integer (no decimals) and
                                 suffixed with “%”.  
       • TOTAL_NET_TRAFFIC_KBPS – the sum of NET_RX_KBPS and NET_TX_KBPS
                                 from both snapshots, rounded to one decimal.  

    4. Produce the report   /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/report.txt
       using **this authoritative format** (every line and separator must match):

            --- SUMMARY ---
            AVG_CPU_USER=<two-decimal number>
            PEAK_DISK_USAGE=<integer>% 
            TOTAL_NET_TRAFFIC_KBPS=<one-decimal number>
            --- DETAIL ---
            <verbatim copy of the entire original snapshot.txt content>

       Notes:  
       • The three metric lines must appear in that exact order.  
       • There is a single space after “---” in the separators.  
       • After the final “--- DETAIL ---” line, copy the
         raw snapshot data exactly as it appears (including the blank line
         that separates the two blocks in snapshot.txt).  
       • Do not add any extra blank lines anywhere else.

    5. When everything is finished, create the flag file
       /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/.done  that contains exactly the word

            OK

       followed by a trailing newline.

    The entire task can be accomplished with standard Linux utilities
    in roughly five to ten commands (mkdir, cat, grep, awk, sed, cut, printf,
    etc.).  Do **not** modify snapshot.txt itself; only read from it.

    The automated test will confirm:
       • The presence of /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/report.txt
         and its byte-for-byte contents.  
       • The presence of /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/.done containing “OK\n”.

    No root privileges are required.  Work only inside /home/user.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the grading harness.
        The agent MUST NOT see anything inside this block.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing directory and file -->
    Directory: /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/raw/

    File: /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/raw/snapshot.txt
    Contents (including the blank line between the two blocks):

TIMESTAMP=2025-04-01T12:30:00Z
CPU_USER=12.5
CPU_SYSTEM=3.1
CPU_IDLE=84.4
MEM_TOTAL=7989
MEM_USED=3120
MEM_FREE=4869
SWAP_TOTAL=2048
SWAP_USED=128
DISK_ROOT_USED_PCT=45
DISK_VAR_USED_PCT=67
DISK_HOME_USED_PCT=38
TOP_PROC_MEM=python3:512M
TOP_PROC_CPU=node:25.3
NET_RX_KBPS=120.5
NET_TX_KBPS=98.7

TIMESTAMP=2025-04-01T12:45:00Z
CPU_USER=15.8
CPU_SYSTEM=4.0
CPU_IDLE=80.2
MEM_TOTAL=7989
MEM_USED=3421
MEM_FREE=4568
SWAP_TOTAL=2048
SWAP_USED=256
DISK_ROOT_USED_PCT=46
DISK_VAR_USED_PCT=70
DISK_HOME_USED_PCT=39
TOP_PROC_MEM=java:640M
TOP_PROC_CPU=python3:27.9
NET_RX_KBPS=150.9
NET_TX_KBPS=110.3


    # Expected values calculated from the above snapshots
    AVG_CPU_USER = (12.5 + 15.8) / 2  -> 14.15
    PEAK_DISK_USAGE = max(45,67,38,46,70,39) -> 70%
    TOTAL_NET_TRAFFIC_KBPS = 120.5 + 98.7 + 150.9 + 110.3 -> 480.4

    # Expected report file
    Must be located at: /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/report.txt
    Exact byte-for-byte contents:

--- SUMMARY ---
AVG_CPU_USER=14.15
PEAK_DISK_USAGE=70%
TOTAL_NET_TRAFFIC_KBPS=480.4
--- DETAIL ---
TIMESTAMP=2025-04-01T12:30:00Z
CPU_USER=12.5
CPU_SYSTEM=3.1
CPU_IDLE=84.4
MEM_TOTAL=7989
MEM_USED=3120
MEM_FREE=4869
SWAP_TOTAL=2048
SWAP_USED=128
DISK_ROOT_USED_PCT=45
DISK_VAR_USED_PCT=67
DISK_HOME_USED_PCT=38
TOP_PROC_MEM=python3:512M
TOP_PROC_CPU=node:25.3
NET_RX_KBPS=120.5
NET_TX_KBPS=98.7

TIMESTAMP=2025-04-01T12:45:00Z
CPU_USER=15.8
CPU_SYSTEM=4.0
CPU_IDLE=80.2
MEM_TOTAL=7989
MEM_USED=3421
MEM_FREE=4568
SWAP_TOTAL=2048
SWAP_USED=256
DISK_ROOT_USED_PCT=46
DISK_VAR_USED_PCT=70
DISK_HOME_USED_PCT=39
TOP_PROC_MEM=java:640M
TOP_PROC_CPU=python3:27.9
NET_RX_KBPS=150.9
NET_TX_KBPS=110.3

    (Note: The final newline after the last line is required.)

    # Expected flag file
    /home/user/ci_cd_diagnostics/.done
    Content:
OK\n
